name: asia_range_london_breakout_xauusd_m5
hypothesis: On XAUUSD M5, the Asia session (server-time 00:00-06:00) high/low acts as liquidity; a breakout during London open (07:00-11:00 server time) with ATR-normalized range filters and a breakout-body confirmation produces asymmetric follow-through.

signals.entry_long: bar_in_session(London, 07:00-11:00) AND close > asia_range_high AND (close - asia_range_high) >= 0.4*ATR(14) AND candle_body >= 1.0*ATR(14) AND first_breakout_of_day
signals.entry_short: bar_in_session(London, 07:00-11:00) AND close < asia_range_low AND (asia_range_low - close) >= 0.4*ATR(14) AND candle_body >= 1.0*ATR(14) AND first_breakout_of_day
signals.range_build: asia_range_high = max(high) / asia_range_low = min(low) over session_mask(00:00-06:00 server time)

rules: skip Mondays first hour and FOMC high-impact days (if calendar available, else always-on)
session_mask enforces both range-build and trade windows use BROKER/server time (not hardcoded UTC)

exits.stop_loss: opposite side of asia range + 0.2*ATR buffer (for long: asia_range_low - 0.2*ATR)
exits.time_stop: close at London session end (11:00 server time) if neither SL/TP hit
exits.breakeven: move SL to entry after +1.0*ATR favorable

implementation_notes: CRITICAL: use broker/server time for session masks and parameterize start/end hours; verify non-empty asia_range on first IS bars; log bars-in-range and breakout-count diagnostics to catch the zero-trades plumbing failure from gen 35.
